concepts.description: loyalty conditional on power; documented breaking

title: High-Density Concepts — Natori-Ryū Operational Framework

concepts.title: Natori-Ryū Operational Doctrine
concepts.description: integrated survival framework: night combat, defense architecture, servant management, road security; pragmatism over honor; tactics over ideology

concepts.title: Conditional Loyalty in Natori Doctrine
concepts.description: loyalty structural not emotional; breaks cleanly when power conditions change; reframes honor-shame framework as loyalty-pragmatism

concepts.title: Death Preparation and Fear Management
concepts.description: psychological readiness as trainable capacity; accepts death as tactical possibility; trains nervous system to function under lethal threat; contrasts with Bushidō mystification

concepts.title: Warrior Embodied Readiness
concepts.description: chi development as nervous system regulation enabling clear perception and rapid response; spiritual practice grounded in physiology not mysticism

concepts.description: kishōmon, shinmon, burning; selective effectiveness

concepts.description: voluntary vs. coercive; double meaning conceals execution as honor

concepts.title: Loyalty Under Coercion
concepts.description: family hostage-taking; coercion-to-internalization mechanism

concepts.title: Sacrifice and Service in the Afterlife
concepts.description: Daoist/Buddhist/Shintō afterlife models; death as job transition

concepts.title: The Way (Dō/Tao)
concepts.description: Daoist-Confucian undergirding; permits contradiction through context-appropriateness

title: Medium-Density Concepts — Natori-Ryū Tactical Doctrine

concepts.title: Samurai Daily Discipline: Kokoroe
concepts.description: structured practice regimen integrating footwork, weapon maintenance, breathing, meditation; discipline as foundation for presence under threat

concepts.title: Defense Architecture in Residential Design
concepts.description: fortification principles embedded in ordinary houses; passage width, wall positioning, secret exits, kill-zones; material design as survival strategy

concepts.title: Night Combat Principles
concepts.description: darkness eliminates visual dominance; audio signature becomes primary signal; positioning advantage replaces strength advantage; psychological preparation for disorientation

concepts.title: Evening Security Protocols
concepts.description: systematic threat assessment at nightfall; sentry positioning, warning signals, retreat routes, defensive positioning; paranoia as reasonable response

concepts.title: Shinobi Infiltration of Fortified Structures
concepts.description: understanding infiltration method as prerequisite for defense; predictable human errors in observation; defense architecture exploiting guard psychology

concepts.title: Ippei Yoko: The Independent Soldier
concepts.description: warrior operating without unit cohesion; self-sufficiency doctrine; survival through positioning and concealment rather than formation

concepts.title: Written Oath and Pledge Integrity
concepts.description: documentation as commitment enforcement; public witnessing; ritual weight of signed pledge; oath-breaking consequences

concepts.title: Road Threats: Highwaymen and Tsujigiri
concepts.description: common dangers on travel routes; deceptive ambush techniques; recognition and avoidance protocols; counter-ambush positioning

concepts.title: Servant and Retainer Management
concepts.description: psychological dynamics of subordinate relationships; authority maintenance; reward/punishment incentive structures; loyalty through clear incentives not sentiment

concepts.description: visible performance of rank through material/behavioral signals

concepts.description: grammar encodes hierarchy; impossibility of non-hierarchical speech

concepts.title: Shameless Killing
concepts.description: arbitrary violence downward permitted; directed-upward violence forbidden

concepts.title: Nagashino and Oda's Innovation
concepts.description: pragmatism overrides tradition when survival at stake

concepts.title: Kusunoki Masashige
concepts.description: reputation as political construct; vilification → rehabilitation → deification

title: Low-Density Concepts — Natori-Ryū Tactical Reference

concepts.title: Natori Masazumi — Biography and Historical Context
concepts.description: 16th-century military strategist; survived multiple regime transitions; documented survivorship doctrine; pragmatism as explicit philosophy

concepts.title: Five Foundations: Goshi Doctrine
concepts.description: five core principles structuring Natori's teaching; foundational elements underlying all tactical doctrine

concepts.title: Skepticism Toward Night Monsters
concepts.description: supernatural experiences as nervous system weakness; rational explanation of combat hallucinations; skepticism as spiritual advancement

concepts.title: Rope Binding and Restraint: Kyosoku no Waza
concepts.description: prisoner restraint technique; three-point binding; knot selection; psychological weight of restraint; logistical application

concepts.title: Throwing and Body Leverage: Nage no Waza Principles
concepts.description: leverage geometry; three leverage systems; speed over power; tactical application in groups and crowded conditions

concepts.title: Pressure Point Targeting: Kyusho no Waza
concepts.description: anatomical vulnerabilities; primary targets; tactical application; precision and limitations; defensive positioning implications

concepts.title: Footwork and Stance Positioning: Ashisabaki and Kamae
concepts.description: stance categories; footwork mechanics; tactical application; training requirements; foundation of all technique

concepts.title: Speed Versus Power: Doctrine and Trade-Offs
concepts.description: speed primary over raw power; temporal advantage; tactical limitations; training implications; integration with broader doctrine

concepts.title: Weapon Maintenance and Readiness: The Warrior's Responsibility
concepts.description: daily maintenance; edge preservation; handle integrity; readiness standards; logistics integration

concepts.title: Voice Projection and Vocal Presence: Kiai and Command
concepts.description: vocalization as tactical tool; breath mechanics; applications in combat and command; psychological effects; limitations

title: Bose — Strategic Leadership Architecture (Phase 4: History-Domain High-Density Synthesis)

concepts.title: Empire as Genetic Drift
concepts.description: empires fragment through accumulated local decisions, not rebellion; decentralized commanders each making locally optimal decisions; drift away from founder's unified system; Alexander's immediate fragmentation vs. Rome's institutional survival; genetic engineering (institutional design) vs. genetic drift (no constraints on local decisions)

concepts.title: Conquest as Psychological Domination
concepts.description: conquest as breaking enemy will, not taking territory; Alexander's visible certainty/impossible speed/visible commitment as psychological overwhelm; breakdown of enemy agency; successor's failure to replicate psychological domination despite military skill; Rome's institutional domination vs. Alexander's personal domination

concepts.title: Strategic Impatience in Historical Pattern
concepts.description: impatience as choice between risk types; Hydaspes (brilliant impatience, organization capacity matches tempo) vs. Hyphasis (destructive impatience, organization capacity exceeded); Rome's sustainable tempo vs. Alexander's exhaustion; organizational capacity as hard constraint on strategy; tempo-capacity matching as prerequisite for durability

concepts.title: Integration as Assimilation
concepts.description: marriage policy as identity erasure; integration (both parties choose to become something new) vs. assimilation (one party forced to abandon identity); voluntary cultural change vs. forced identity dissolution; long-term outcomes of integration (Sufi-Hindu synthesis 500+ years) vs. assimilation (Spanish conquest language erasure in 3 generations); Ottoman millet system as alternative

concepts.title: The Founder Problem in Historical Perspective
concepts.description: universal pattern: visionary movements collapse with founder (Jesus, Genghis Khan, Napoleon, Alexander); institutional systems survive founder (Rome, Church post-300CE); movement = founder-dependent; institution = principle-dependent; transformation from movement to institution takes 300+ years (Jesus → Church); quality of succession irrelevant if organization is movement not institution

title: Population Displacement & Transformation Dynamics (Stone Age Herbalist / *Skull Cults and Corpse Brides: Essays Vol. II*, 2023)

concepts.title: Corded Ware De-Neolithisation
concepts.description: Corded Ware pastoralists abandoning agriculture for fisher-forager subsistence in eastern Baltic ~2800-2400 BCE; pottery residue chemistry vs. human bone isotope contradictions; maritime expansion; genetic evidence continuity; burial practice shifts (supine male orientation, kurgans); persistence of cultural difference despite mixed ancestry; reversal of standard farmer-victory narrative

concepts.title: Wine as Paradox — Domestication Enabling Ecstasy
concepts.description: Wine paradox: domesticated product enabling transgression; dual-origin hypotheses unresolved (Thracian vs. Minoan); grape domestication ~4500 BCE (Dikili Tash chemical evidence); wine elevation to elite prestige drink; Dionysus divinity origins contested; ritual transgression as social resilience mechanism; what societies lose without controlled wildness

concepts.title: Anglo-Saxon Migration — Genetics Vindication
concepts.description: Anglo-Saxon population replacement vindicated by 2023 genetic studies (60-90% ancestry); hypothesis rejected in 1960s-2010s academic consensus; now confirmed through paleogenomics despite decades of scholarly hostility; reveals how moral clarity about pseudoscience can produce blind spots; technology-mediated access enabling contested knowledge reversal

concepts.title: Māori Genocide of Moriori
concepts.description: Moriori chose pacifism (Nunuku's Law) and faced extinction by musket-armed Māori; moral coherence vs. survival as incompatible goods; technology (muskets) created vulnerability that culture (commitment to non-violence) could not overcome; pacifism survives only when all populations are pacifist

concepts.title: Farmer-Forager Contact Dynamics — Multiple Outcomes
concepts.description: Three divergent outcomes from farmer-forager contact (Corded Ware adoption of foraging in Baltic, Drakensberg San-Bantu hybridity, Mediterranean coexistence); outcomes depend on ecology, technology transfer, cultural choice—not on agricultural superiority as universal principle; conquest outcomes contingent on multiple variables

concepts.title: Hmong Statelessness and Diaspora Rupture
concepts.description: Statelessness as strategic advantage for centuries (highland autonomy from lowland Southeast Asian states); catastrophic vulnerability in 20th century with nation-states and total warfare; American War in Vietnam creating refugee crisis; diaspora communities struggling to maintain culture in embedded position within foreign states; time-scale inversion (advantage becomes disadvantage)

title: Spanish Conquest of Mexico (Townsend / *The Fifth Sun: A New History of the Aztecs*, 2023)

concepts.title: Conquest as Negotiated Process
concepts.description: conquest contingent on three indigenous choices: Marina's translation enabling Spanish comprehension; Tlaxcaltec alliance determining military advantage; Moctezuma's negotiation strategy enabling Spanish exploitation of coalition fractures; Spanish victory real but path-dependent on indigenous decisions

concepts.title: Marina (Malinche) — Translator as Indispensable Agent
concepts.description: translation labor absolutely essential to Spanish conquest; systematic erasure from Spanish narratives reveals how translator labor is made invisible; maintains Spanish narrative of autonomous genius; Marina's invisibility serves ideological function

concepts.title: Smallpox as Force Multiplier
concepts.description: reframes disease as condition enabling conquest through indigenous crisis management paralysis, not deterministic cause; cascading crises (succession, labor disruption, burial obligations) simultaneously affecting multiple systems; indigenous institutions designed for continuity, not crisis recovery

concepts.title: Moctezuma's Pragmatism
concepts.description: recovers Moctezuma as pragmatic calculator, not fatalistic believer; negotiation strategy rational response to radical uncertainty, unprecedented military threat, institutional constraints; Spanish profiting from that rationality enables Spanish to exploit coalition fractures

concepts.title: Xiuhpohualli — 52-Year Calendar
concepts.description: indigenous cyclical time-framework foundational to Nahua historical consciousness; 52-year calendar made conquest appear as disruption within cycle rather than apocalyptic conclusion; continuing use of calendar was refusal to accept Spanish temporal frame

concepts.title: Altepetl — Indigenous Political Units
concepts.description: city-state as persistent political unit surviving conquest; Spanish preserved altepetl because functionally efficient for colonial administration; shows how indigenous institutional structures became foundation of colonial power

concepts.title: Calpolli — Decentralized Administration
concepts.description: kinship-based labor organization as remarkably efficient administrative technology; Spanish preserved calpolli despite being designed for indigenous benefit because it worked; kinship authority internalized obligations without external force

concepts.title: Tlatoani — Paramount Chief
concepts.description: paramount chief as negotiator, not autocrat; preserved elite status through negotiating position within Spanish colonial hierarchy; demonstrates pragmatic collaboration as rational choice for status preservation

concepts.title: Flower Wars (Xochiyaoyotl)
concepts.description: ritualized conflict as sophisticated political communication system; Spanish refusal to follow flower war protocols prevented indigenous ability to communicate military capacity; what appears as weakness (accepting ritual framework) was diplomatic sophistication

concepts.title: Chimalpahin — Annals and Indigenous Historiography
concepts.description: indigenous historian as political actor using annals to assert indigenous claims; archive as resistance strategy preserving indigenous knowledge against erasure; documentation was temporal assertion — we persist, time continues

title: Stone Age Herbalist — Berserkers, Cannibals & Shamans (2022)

concepts.title: Aztec Metaphysics — Teotl
concepts.description: Nahuatl monist cosmology: the universe as single dynamic process (teotl); three motion-changes (malinalli/tlaloc/quetzalcoatl); sacrifice as cosmic maintenance; Maffie's philosophical reconstruction; why the human sacrifice makes internal logical sense

concepts.title: Secret Societies and the Biology of Hierarchy
concepts.description: Hayden's 12-feature cluster across all cultures; knowledge restriction as core mechanism; dysgenic shaman thesis (most disturbing individual leads hierarchy); koryos/Männerbund Indo-European war-band institution; Harii as Germanic example

concepts.title: Chinese Filial Cannibalism
concepts.description: gegu practice (slicing own flesh to feed sick parent); four types of Chinese cannibalism (medical, filial, revenge, gourmet); Guangxi Massacre 1966-76 as modern instance; xiao (filial piety) as cosmological driver; victim passivity as data point

concepts.title: Human Sacrifice in the Modern World
concepts.description: ongoing sacrifice in Uganda (one child/week minimum, 2012), India (Aghori Tantric priests as common denominator), UK (Victoria Climbié, Adam Thames torso, Kristy Bamu, kindoki/exorcism framework); three distinct cosmological logics; muti/juju vs. deity-appeasement vs. kindoki frameworks; why legal deterrence fails

title: ALMOST-READY HUBS (4-5 pages, clear coherence, ready for MOC build)

concepts.title: Shamanism in Early Civilizations
concepts.description: 5 pages: shamanic practice and cosmology in neolithic through early state societies; shamanism as state-forming technology and psychological framework; Hongshan, Aztec, Siberian contexts

concepts.title: Cannibalism: Extreme Survival and Ritual Context
concepts.description: 4 pages: documented cannibalism across ritual, survival, medical, and filial contexts; forensic evidence and historical reconstruction; Franklin Expedition, Chinese filial piety, ritual sacrifice

concepts.title: Extinction & Civilizational Collapse
concepts.description: 4 pages: historical cases of demographic collapse and cultural extinction; Parsi Zoroastrianism, Tasmanian Aboriginal dietary collapse, Moriori genocide, Marori impact patterns

title: Guerrilla Warfare & Counterinsurgency (Boot / *Invisible Armies*, 2013)

concepts.title: Guerrilla Warfare — Definition and Origins
concepts.description: Boot's typology (guerrilla/terrorism/insurgency); apolitical-tribal vs. ideological-political distinction; Eastern Way of War debunking; 5,000-year sweep

concepts.title: Guerrilla Paradox — Weak Beats Strong
concepts.description: 25.5% success rate; Huntington's formula; survival as primary weapon; paradox of survival tactics ≠ victory tactics; asymmetry of costs

concepts.title: Terrorism vs. Guerrilla Warfare
concepts.description: noncombatant targeting criterion; means/ends matrix; Boot's three-part typology; Islamist internal debate on civilian targeting

concepts.title: Guerrilla Warfare as Historical Norm
concepts.description: irregular war as statistical majority; conventional war as exception; structural explanation; 5,000-year database

concepts.title: Apolitical/Tribal vs. Ideological/Political Insurgency
concepts.description: pre-modern tribal default vs. post-1776 ideological shift; narrative war requirement; COIN failure from conflating types

concepts.title: Insurgent Success Rates — Statistics Database
concepts.description: 25.5% overall; 40.3% post-1945; 10-year average duration; era breakdown; correlation variables; Hobsbawm methodology limits

concepts.title: Counterinsurgency Doctrine — Ancient Origins
concepts.description: Assyrian/Roman terror-and-co-optation; Byzantine Strategikon; colonial development; repeating knowledge-loss cycle

concepts.title: Population-Centric COIN
concepts.description: Galula/Thompson synthesis; hearts-and-minds misreading; Malaya success; Vietnam failure; Petraeus/Iraq Surge

concepts.title: Legitimacy as Critical Factor
concepts.description: nationalism as most durable claim; foreign-government trap; post-1945 decolonization shift; population's protection-bet calculation

concepts.title: Galula and Thompson — COIN Doctrine
concepts.description: Galula's 80/20 formula + four phases; Thompson's rule-of-law and five principles; convergences; divergences (sequencing, legalism)

concepts.title: FM 3-24 Field Manual
concepts.description: the 2006 document that rediscovered COIN; paradoxes; clear-hold-build; population as center of gravity; limits

concepts.title: Byzantine Strategikon — COIN Manual
concepts.description: ca. 600 CE; political over military solutions; local allies; mobile response; intelligence priority; convergence with Arthashastra

concepts.title: Counterinsurgent Literacy Advantage
concepts.description: four-stage documentation failure cycle; British/French/US cases; career incentive structure; why institutions don't preserve knowledge

concepts.title: Rogers's Rangers — Tactical Doctrine
concepts.description: 28 Rules of Ranging (1757); first written American irregular doctrine; documentation as the innovation; lineage to Special Forces

concepts.title: Outside Support as Success Factor
concepts.description: highest-correlation database variable; arms/safe havens/money/diplomatic cover; American Revolution, Vietcong, mujahideen, Hezbollah cases

concepts.title: Conventional Tactics Fail Against Unconventional Threat
concepts.description: target identification problem; kill-count fallacy; search-and-destroy vs. clear-and-hold; M-16 vs. F-16 principle

concepts.title: Mass Terror — COIN Limitations
concepts.description: conditions where terror works (rare); modern incompatibility; grievance multiplication; Nazi Yugoslavia, French Algeria failures

concepts.title: Public Opinion as Crucial Factor
concepts.description: post-1776 hinge; democratic accountability; technology amplification; insurgency as media operation; Tet case

concepts.title: Long Duration — Timeframe Factor
concepts.description: 10-year average; 14-year post-1945; time asymmetry; why time doesn't automatically favor guerrilla; quick-victory temptation costs

concepts.title: Leadership Decapitation — When It Works
concepts.description: conditions (cult-of-personality, early-stage, tribal); hydra regeneration; martyrdom multiplication; franchise resilience

concepts.title: Technology in Low-Intensity Conflict
concepts.description: paradox of irrelevant superiority; printing press to social media as decisive tech; nuclear irrelevance; WMD future risk

concepts.title: Media War — The Insurgent's Second Battlefield
concepts.description: Tet as military victory/narrative defeat; three mechanisms (atrocity amplification, time asymmetry, spectacle); Zawahiri doctrine; counterinsurgent's structural disadvantage

concepts.title: Chevauchée — Medieval Irregular Warfare
concepts.description: Black Prince's systematic devastation; economic warfare logic; three objectives; strong-party irregular logic

concepts.title: Social Bandits — Hobsbawm's Primitive Rebels
concepts.description: noble robber typology; pre-political precursor; Haiti/China/Mexico examples

concepts.title: Al-Qaeda Franchise Model
concepts.description: pre-9/11 centralized vs. post-2001 franchise; ideological self-sufficiency; brand without control; ISIS meme-insurgency variant

concepts.title: Zarqawi's Strategic Failure
concepts.description: sectarian strategy; Zawahiri letter; Sunni Awakening as self-created COIN; franchise autonomy as strategic liability

concepts.title: Scythians vs. Darius — Nomadic Warfare
concepts.description: 513 BCE; earliest "unpunchable enemy" case; refuse engagement; attrit through logistics; survival as primary weapon

concepts.title: Beth Horon — Roman-Jewish Revolt (66 CE)
concepts.description: tactical victory as strategic trap; Twelfth Legion destroyed; guerrilla paradox compressed; Roman mass terror response

concepts.title: American Colonial Indian Wars
concepts.description: skulking way of war; Metacom's War; Braddock's defeat; ranger tradition genesis

concepts.title: Scottish Jacobite Rebellions
concepts.description: apolitical-tribal insurgency in Western Europe; Culloden; cultural destruction as COIN; Clearances demographic transformation

concepts.title: Haitian Revolution
concepts.description: most improbable victory; Toussaint L'Ouverture; absolute legitimacy asymmetry; biological multiplier; Napoleon's catastrophe

concepts.title: Peninsular War — Etymology of "Guerrilla"
concepts.description: origin of the word; French occupation; Wellington's conventional-guerrilla partnership; Mina and El Empecinado; Goya's media war

concepts.title: Greek War of Independence
concepts.description: nationalist legitimacy construction; philhellene movement; Navarino; Great Power intervention as decisive variable

concepts.title: Garibaldi — Italian Unification
concepts.description: *Mille* expedition; charismatic leadership as force multiplier; founding myth construction; Cavour's diplomatic preparation

concepts.title: Imam Shamil — Caucasus Insurgency
concepts.description: 30-year resistance; imamate state-building; Islamic law as governance; deforestation/resettlement response

concepts.title: First Afghan War
concepts.description: Kabul retreat (1842); Brydon sole survivor; structural conditions for Afghan resistance; no central authority to defeat

concepts.title: Pashtun Northwest Frontier
concepts.description: century-long British management; Pashtunwali structure; egalitarian tribal system; Political Service model

concepts.title: Lyautey in Morocco
concepts.description: tache d'huile (oil spot) doctrine; build ahead of fight; work with existing authority; Rif War limits

concepts.title: Boer War — Commandos
concepts.description: *kommando* system; marksmanship/mobility; scorched earth; concentration camps; public opinion turning point

concepts.title: Lawrence and the Arab Revolt
concepts.description: rail interdiction strategy; presence not positions; Aqaba operation; Sykes-Picot betrayal; external support as trap

concepts.title: Orde Wingate — Special Forces
concepts.description: Special Night Squads (Palestine); Gideon Force (Ethiopia); Chindits (Burma); individual innovation vs. institutional conservatism

concepts.title: Tito's Yugoslav Partisans
concepts.description: multi-ethnic Brotherhood and Unity; seven German offensives survived; proto-state governance; Chetnik collaboration failure

concepts.title: Mao and the Chinese Revolution
concepts.description: three-phase people's war; Long March; Yan'an base area; political work as military work; Three Rules of Discipline

concepts.title: Dien Bien Phu
concepts.description: Phase 2→3 transition; French intelligence failure; artillery through jungle; political collapse after military defeat

concepts.title: Malaya — COIN Success
concepts.description: Briggs Plan; New Villages; Templer's governance-military integration; independence as COIN instrument; intelligence from population

concepts.title: Algeria — French Failure
concepts.description: torture producing intelligence and destroying legitimacy simultaneously; Galula's district as positive case inside failure; OAS mutiny; FLN media war

concepts.title: Lansdale — Philippines
concepts.description: Huk rebellion; Magsaysay as model political partner; EDCOR exit ramp; governance not firepower

concepts.title: Lansdale — Vietnam
concepts.description: Diem as inadequate partner; Strategic Hamlets failure; same advisor, wrong government; COIN advisory limits

concepts.title: Cuban Revolution
concepts.description: Sierra Maestra; Batista's legitimacy collapse; army that wouldn't fight; NYT media strategy

concepts.title: Che Guevara — Foco Theory Failure
concepts.description: foco theory; Bolivia conditions mismatch; no peasant support; Mao vs. Guevara sequencing dispute; Bolivia death

concepts.title: Vietnam War — Firepower Limitations
concepts.description: body count fallacy; search-and-destroy vs. clear-and-hold; CORDS and Phoenix; Tet and narrative collapse

concepts.title: Petraeus and the Iraq Surge
concepts.description: FM 3-24 field test; JSSs embedded in neighborhoods; Sons of Iraq; dramatic violence reduction; strategic limits; ISIS as sequel

title: Maratha State-Building (Purandare / *Shivaji: India's Great Warrior King*, 2019)

concepts.title: Maratha Guerrilla Warfare Doctrine
concepts.description: five operating principles (intelligence-first, surprise over force, terrain-as-weapon, speed-as-size, scorched-earth); Bahirji Naik's intelligence layer as enabling infrastructure; 23 forts recaptured in 4 months

concepts.title: Maratha Maritime Power and Naval Strategy
concepts.description: galbat fleet design (40-70 tons, optimized for Konkan coast); Sindhudurg island fort (48 acres, 52 bastions, 1 crore hons); Surat loot → naval construction financing loop; multi-community command (Daulat Khan, Mainak Bhandari)

concepts.title: Maratha Fort Architecture and Defensive Strategy
concepts.description: havaldar/sabnis/sarnobat triad (keys always with havaldar); mixed-caste appointments as anti-treachery design; Rajgad three-machi redundancy; 60+ forts as distributed sovereignty network; 23 forts surrendered and recaptured

concepts.title: Maratha Administrative Governance Model
concepts.description: batai crop-share system replacing bighawani; jagir abolition; direct soldier payment; watandar wada demolition; advance credit to new farmers; Annaji Datto land survey; Ashta Pradhans with Sanskrit titles

concepts.title: Jijabai's Formation Role
concepts.description: maternal formation through Ramayana/Mahabharata narrative; Agra regency; land grants issued in her name; public confrontation of Netaji Palkar and Siddi Hilal; "the real chhatra of the Chhatrapati"; death 11 days after coronation

concepts.title: Territorial Sovereignty vs. Vassal Submission
concepts.description: mansab refusal at Agra (given to 9-yr-old Sambhaji instead); court outburst; basket escape; post-escape submission letter as strategic de-escalation; "four steps forward, two steps back" doctrine

concepts.title: Maratha Intelligence and Spy Network
concepts.description: Bahirji Naik as named spy-chief; Surat pre-raid reconnaissance; Afzal Khan camp intelligence (no heavy artillery confirmed); Lal Mahal layout knowledge; Agra basket conditioning as behavioral intelligence operation

concepts.title: Maratha Coalition-Building and Alliance Politics
concepts.description: Kanhoji Jedhe loyalty-testing; Chhatrasal Bundela (advised to build own kingdom, not join); Golconda tribute (1 lakh hons/yr); British EIC treaty (10,000 rupees); Portuguese chauthai; Treaty of Hyderabad before southern campaign

concepts.title: Hindu Identity as Political Legitimacy
concepts.description: Sanskrit titles replacing Persian; Rajya Shaka new era; coins "Raja ShivaChhatrapati"; Gaga Bhatt from Kashi certifying Sisodia/Kshatriya lineage; Saptakoteshwara temple reconstruction (Goa, 1668); operates simultaneously with religious pluralism

concepts.title: Chhatrapati Coronation as Political Act
concepts.description: unprecedented sovereign declaration inside Mughal empire; Henry Oxenden eyewitness; four political dimensions (treaty legitimacy / counter-Aurangzeb / Kshatriya assertion / civilizational claim); Machiavellian founder transition

concepts.title: Maratha Economic Dimensions — Chauth and Revenue Reform
concepts.description: chauth as sovereignty-acknowledgment mechanism; Surat I (1-1.5 crore rupees) → Sindhudurg capital; batai replacing bighawani; direct treasury payment; fort upkeep economics (10,000 hons/yr per sea fort)

concepts.title: Strategic Patience and Calibrated Retreat
concepts.description: "be not the flash in the pan"; 8-yr Jaawali wait; Sinhagad surrendered for Shahaji; Treaty of Purandar (23 forts); 3-yr quiet phase after Agra; "four steps forward, two steps back" doctrine; tension with death-resignation

concepts.title: Justice as Political Legitimacy
concepts.description: Ranjhe village ruling (zero-tolerance as loyalty-building); Keshri Singh's mother in Shivaji's palanquin; British prisoners released with "let us forget the past"; Khandoji Khopde calibrated punishment; Mughal prisoners freed with provisions

concepts.title: Military Code of Conduct as State Doctrine
concepts.description: seven prohibitions (no women in army / no adultery / no capture of civilian women/children / no molesting cows or Brahmins / take only by purchase in enemy territory / no burning houses); Khafi Khan (hostile Mughal historian) corroboration: "injunctions upon this point were very strict"

concepts.title: Diplomatic Correspondence as Statecraft
concepts.description: Afzal Khan false-submission letter; post-escape conciliatory letter to Aurangzeb; mockery letter to Mughal officials after Lal Mahal; April 1679 jaziya letter (theological register); register shifts completely by audience

title: Mass Communication and Propaganda (Bernays / *The Edward Bernays Reader*, 2021)

concepts.title: War Propaganda Doctrine
concepts.description: Lasswell's six domestic propaganda factors + Bruntz's five international categories; Germany/Britain/US comparative analysis; three-objective framework (own morale / enemy morale / neutral morale); 1942 psychological general staff prescription; democratic-totalitarian convergence problem

title: Vietnam War and Media Propaganda (Chomsky & Herman, 1988)

concepts.title: Tet Offensive Media Coverage
concepts.description: 1968; Major media case: coverage was accurate within unchallenged bounds; Freedom House Braestrup study shows media reported facts correctly but within "US defending democracy" frame; public opinion stayed stable despite accurate reporting because the frame wasn't questioned; proves that accuracy ≠ truth-telling when frame is false

concepts.title: Tonkin Gulf Incident Media Coverage
concepts.description: August 1964; first attack real, second attack false; media reported both as fact; government was available/official/authoritative source; independent verification was expensive/risky; journalist followed professional standards and reported false official claim; Pentagon Papers later revealed falsity

concepts.title: Paris Peace Agreements Misinterpretation
concepts.description: January 1973; Kissinger's interpretation contradicted actual treaty text; media reported Kissinger's version as truth; journalist trusted official interpretation over document reading; authority bias case at institutional level

concepts.title: Vietnam War Institutional Narrative
concepts.description: Meta-analysis; US framing as "defending democracy against communism"; actual history shows US blocked 1956 elections that Ho would have won, prevented negotiations, destroyed democratic alternatives; media operated accurately within false premise; bounds-of-controversy analysis

concepts.title: KGB-Bulgarian Pope Shooting Plot
concepts.description: 1981 attempt on Pope John Paul II; media accepted US/Western narrative as conspiracy theory; Sterling-Henze-Kalb model construction; Agca incoherence and coaching evidence; Rome trial acquitted the accused; media ignored trial acquittal and continued accepting narrative; Western geopolitical motives never examined

concepts.title: Guatemala 1984 Election Observation
concepts.description: Elections under military rule; media reported as legitimate democratic process; military prevented opposition candidate participation, intimidated population, determined outcomes; media accuracy about vote counts masked frame falsity; bounds-of-controversy prevented questions about military control

title: Stoic

concepts.title: Stoic Dichotomy of Control
concepts.description: eph' hēmin / ta ektos; hegemonikon as the only thing properly "yours"; four-tradition governing-faculty convergence (Stoic/Igbo/Bansenshukai/Trika); Marcus Aurelius primary text

concepts.title: Stoic Daily Practice
concepts.description: Stoic insight is not a durable acquisition; morning preparation; motivated action; return after failure; "polish yourself day after day" (three-tradition parallel with Bansenshukai and Metsuke)

concepts.title: Impermanence and Temporal Perspective
concepts.description: Marcus Aurelius "view from above"; temporal and nature-as-change moves; promontory image; cross-domain convergence with Eastern *anicca* and modern CBT/ACT

title: Strategic Thinking (Welch / *The Strategist Code*)

concepts.title: Strategic Thinking — Definition and Framework
concepts.description: four thinker types (status-quo/Carteaux, reactive/Hood, wait-react-defend, proactive strategist); purpose as starting point; long time horizon (Banfield [VERIFIED]); tension navigation as competency; meditative type

concepts.title: Decisive Point and Leverage
concepts.description: Clausewitz: concentrate everything on the decisive point, remove forces from secondary fronts; Napoleon at Toulon as worked example; seizing initiative; simplicity principle; leverage as structural principle

concepts.title: History as Strategic Resource
concepts.description: Napoleon's dual modes (master/teacher vs. servant/rhetorical tool); Nietzsche's three uses (grounding, inspiration, illumination); analogical reasoning disciplines (avoid fighting the last war, surface assumptions, multiple analogies); pothos — Alexander's longing to surpass even the gods

title: Movement History (Wilson / *How to Take Over the World*, 2026)

concepts.title: Shadow Governance Infrastructure
concepts.description: NSDAP "state within a state": shadow ministries built before power arrived; slide-in transition; capacity vs. position distinction; cross-domain: sadhana/tapas (eastern-spirituality), worldbuilding-as-foundation (creative-practice)

title: Machiavellian Political Philosophy (Wilson / *How to Take Over the World*, 2025)

concepts.title: Machiavellian Realpolitik
concepts.description: Political science as value-neutral biology: describes how power actually works without moral filter; Machiavelli as first modern political scientist; two Machiavellis (*Prince* vs. *Discourses*)

concepts.title: Machiavellian Glory Hierarchy
concepts.description: Explicit ranking: founders of religions > founders of republics/kingdoms > military leaders > men of letters > professionals; reveals Machiavelli's normative commitments beneath the amoral surface

title: Arthashastra (Trautmann scholarly interpretation of Kangle translation, 2012)

concepts.title: Arthashastra — Kingship and the Rajarshi Ideal
concepts.description: King as taxer/arbiter/keeper of order/entrepreneur; rajarshi (king-sage) as secular asceticism; nishkama karma parallel [PLAUSIBLE]; exhausting daily schedule as structural anti-corruption; four instruments (sama/dana/bheda/danda)

concepts.title: Kingdom vs. Republic in the Arthashastra
concepts.description: Mechanical solidarity (sangha/republics) vs. organic solidarity (kingdoms); republics formidable but defeatable via bheda; kingdoms prevail economically through division of labor; Durkheim parallel [SYNTHESIS]

concepts.title: Bhaga — The Co-Sharing Model
concepts.description: King as co-sharer not despotic owner; shad-bhagin (the sixth-taker); family/partnership revenue vocabulary; refutes Oriental Despotism thesis [SCHOLARLY — contested]; mutual interest as load-bearing ethical logic

concepts.title: Arthashastra — Market Philosophy
concepts.description: Just price vs. market price; fair profit proportional to distance/risk; transparent pre-transaction price proclamation; counter-cyclical royal price buffering; transparency as information-asymmetry remedy

concepts.title: Arthashastra — Goods and Valuation
concepts.description: Treasure vs. necessity two-category system; India-Rome coral-pearls trade as dating evidence; war elephants/horses as strategic assets; luxury as diplomatic/social tool; pottery/local services as invisible goods

concepts.title: Arthashastra — State Enterprises
concepts.description: Economic zones in priority order (farms/pastures/mines/forests/workshops); state as entrepreneur; sharecropping/wage labor on royal land; mine monopoly rationale; constrained-labor textile workforce; guild system under state licensing

concepts.title: Arthashastra — Law and the Two Courts
concepts.description: Dharmastha (civil, 3-judge panel, 18 vyavahara transaction categories) vs. pradeshtri (criminal, proactive "removal of thorns," market inspection, 1,000-pana collusion fine); complaint-driven vs. proactive detection logic

title: Japanese Warrior Tradition (Cleary Anthology + Ratti/Westbrook)

concepts.title: Just War Typology — Japanese Warrior Tradition
concepts.description: three warfare categories (just/prestige/greed) with 7 specific just-war types; eagerness to mobilize = diagnostic that warfare is predatory; four-element hierarchy with justice first (justice > technique > courage > knowledge); five army types; Naganuma Muneyoshi

concepts.title: Samurai Governance Philosophy
concepts.description: governance as applied character development at scale; five-area framework; Arthashastra convergence table; dojo as governance microcosm (sensei authority mirrors clan hierarchy); Allied occupation exploitation (MacArthur through rather than against the loyalty structure)

concepts.title: Feudal Japan Economic Critique — Kumazawa Banzan
concepts.description: three structural causes transfer wealth from warriors/farmers to merchants: river/sea-route cities foster extravagance, currency replacing rice removes price-stabilizing anchor, proliferation of wants outpaces fixed stipends; structural deterioration invisible during general prosperity; Kumazawa Banzan (1619–1691)

concepts.title: Ryu — Japan's Knowledge Transmission Machine
concepts.description: 159 schools (1843 census); 6 school types; hiden secrecy doctrine (commercial + strategic function); two-phase vitality/formalism cycle (vital necessity → Tokugawa formalist decay); musha-shugyo as anti-stagnation mechanism; creator vs. transmitter sensei distinction

concepts.title: Bushi Social Ecology
concepts.description: shi-nō-kō-shō hierarchy vs. actual martial practice distribution; heimin parallel training tradition; ronin typology (400,000 estimate; wave-man etymology; three groups; bujutsu innovator role); sōhei warrior-monks; merchant combat centers; Tokugawa stratification contradictions

concepts.title: Ninjutsu — Art of Shadow Warfare
concepts.description: jonin/chunin/genin hierarchy; yubijutsu/koppo/saiminjutsu; trickster vs. expert distinction; death/secrecy protocols; deliberate mythology as psychological operations; documentation reliability caveats throughout [POPULAR SOURCE — documentation contested]

title: Late Imperial Russia / Romanov Collapse (Moynahan 1997 + Radzinsky 2000/2001)

concepts.title: Starets Institution — Spiritual Surrender
concepts.description: elder-disciple bond as totalizing authority structure; Optina Pustyn tradition; **STRANNIK CORRECTION** (Radzinsky): Rasputin operated as strannik not starets — see strannik page; slot exploitability

concepts.title: Strannik Wanderer Tradition
concepts.description: **(NEW 2026-04-24)** Russian wandering holy man as distinct authority form; strannik vs. starets distinction (the misidentification in all popular accounts); road as credential; liminal authority without institutional accountability; slot predated the individual

concepts.title: Khlyst Theology — Sin as Redemption Fuel
concepts.description: radical antinomian theology: sin as prerequisite for grace; radenie as "spiritual gymnasium" (Radzinsky first-hand 1964 account); Zhukovskaya seduction script mechanics; Consistory investigation inconclusive finding documented

concepts.title: Russian Popular Religion Underground
concepts.description: **(NEW 2026-04-24)** ecology of underground sects (Khlysty, Skoptsy, Dukhobory, Old Believers); structural conditions generating the shadow; Consistory investigation as Contrary Employment case; official religion producing its own underground

concepts.title: Peasant Authenticity Fantasy — Romanov Variant
concepts.description: ruling-class need for uncredentialed holy peasant; Nicholas II's narod theology; the fantasy destroys its object by making it politically useful

concepts.title: Hemophilia as Political Infrastructure
concepts.description: Alexei's clotting disorder as political infrastructure; Spala 1912; medical-political loop; **psychosomatic mechanism** (Radzinsky): Alexandra calm → Alexei calm → improved hemostasis; aspirin contraindication; diplomatic secrecy costs

concepts.title: Perelom — Regime Tipping Point
concepts.description: psychological threshold at which regime loses sacral protection; lightning rod removal; 60-day sequence to February Revolution

concepts.title: Okhrana Surveillance Architecture
concepts.description: six-agent surveillance team; surveillance-without-solution paradox; **three-way weaponization** (Radzinsky): protection + Dzhunkovsky dossier + Badmaev/Manasevich factional leverage

concepts.title: Petitioner Economy — Parallel State
concepts.description: 300-400 daily visitors; Djanumova ethnographic account; **named cast** (Radzinsky File): Badmaev, Manasevich-Manuilov, Andronikov, Simanovich; Stürmer appointment as petitioner economy at highest altitude

concepts.title: Ministerial Leapfrog — Regime Instability
concepts.description: Purishkevich's coinage; sub-6-month tenure; **Stürmer appointment anatomy** (Radzinsky): Manasevich → night meetings → Pitirim note → Rasputin endorsement → imperial appointment

concepts.title: Fixer and Blackmail Architecture
concepts.description: Andronnikov (access broker) vs. Manuilov (information broker); self-reproducing dependency loop; blackmail equilibrium

concepts.title: Alexandra and Wartime Shadow Governance
concepts.description: letter-telephone-Rasputin relay as governance mechanism; cabinet purge sequence; German sympathy question (unresolved); intimate vs. institutional shadow governance

concepts.title: Rasputin: Political Myth Manufacturing
concepts.description: **(NEW 2026-04-24)** two myth factories (émigré-martyrdom + Soviet-degeneracy) producing compatible fabrications; Schyogolev/Tolstoy 1927 forgeries; constable testimony suppressed by irrelevance; Philippe precedent as structural slot proof

concepts.title: Rasputin's Political Instinct
concepts.description: **(NEW 2026-04-24)** petitioner economy as intelligence system; Balkan War intervention; proto-Bolshevik proposals; Bonch-Bruevich June 1914 article; Gilliard mirror observation; tension between genuine instinct and reflection

concepts.title: Charismatic Healer as Court Slot
concepts.description: **(NEW 2026-04-24)** Philippe precedent (1901–02) proves slot predated man; five-stage slot-generation mechanism; authentication burden; visibility paradox; cross-cultural recurrence

title: Sun Tzu / Art of War (Giles translation, primary-text)

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Five Factors and Strategic Calculation
concepts.description: Temple calculations; Moral Law/Tao, Heaven, Earth, Commander, Method & Discipline; seven comparative questions; calculation as foundation of adaptation

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Deception and Formlessness
concepts.description: "All warfare is based on deception" as epistemological principle; water metaphor (no constant form = not exploitable); deception inventory; do not repeat winning tactics; simulated disorder postulates perfect discipline

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Victory Without Fighting
concepts.description: Hierarchy: attack plans > prevent alliances > attack armies > besiege cities; taking-intact principle (enemy resources transfer to victor); know yourself/know enemy (four-dimension formula); five essentials for victory; sovereign interference as structural disabler

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Shi, Energy, and Zheng/Qi
concepts.description: *Shi* as stored momentum/potential (crossbow metaphor); zheng/qi (direct/indirect) as combinatorial engine generating inexhaustible tactical variation; five-note/five-color analogy; combined energy not individual heroism

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Xu/Shi, Emptiness and Fullness
concepts.description: Initiative principle (first in field = fresh for fight); imposing will vs. having it imposed; concentrate where enemy is empty; appear where enemy must defend; force revelation through provocation; water metaphor in full

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — The Economics of War
concepts.description: 100,000-man campaign cost calculation; "no prolonged warfare benefiting a country"; forage principle (1 cartload enemy = 20 own); conquered foe's resources transfer to victor; speed as economic principle

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Field Intelligence and Signal Reading
concepts.description: Signal grammar of dust clouds, bird flight, soldier posture, camp behavior; behavioral tells (muttering = command crisis; horses eaten = food shortage); passive intelligence vs. active spy system; leakage as organizational broadcast

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — The Commander
concepts.description: Five virtues (wisdom/sincerity/benevolence/courage/strictness); five dangerous faults (recklessness/cowardice/hasty temper/delicacy of honor/over-solicitude); wind/forest/fire/mountain movement principles; soldiers-as-sons relational philosophy; irreversibility principle (anger passes; kingdoms do not)

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Nine Situations and Desperate Ground
concepts.description: Nine ground types (dispersive through desperate) organized by retreat cost; desperate ground psychology (no exit = maximum cohesion); Shuai-jan snake metaphor; cohesion increases with penetration depth; proclaim hopelessness

concepts.title: Sun Tzu — Intelligence and the Five Spies
concepts.description: Foreknowledge as supreme faculty (cost-of-ignorance argument); five spy types (local/inward/converted/doomed/surviving); converted spy as intelligence hub; foreknowledge cannot be obtained inductively or deductively — only from men; Li Ch'uan's roving agent; compartmentalization doctrine

title: Chinese Military Theory (Sawyer, *The Tao of Deception*, 2007)

concepts.title: Chinese Military Theory — Historical Development
concepts.description: 3,000-year periodization; Hsü Tung's Northern Sung synthesis (*Hu-ch'ien Ching*); *Wu-ching Tsung-yao* as institutional compendium; Contrary Employment as epistemological culmination; divination arc; T'ai-pai Yin-ching inversion; Boot tension

concepts.title: Strategic Ethics in Chinese Military Thought
concepts.description: virtue (te) as operational prerequisite not moral prohibition; Questions and Replies most systematic treatment; dual-register framework (civil virtue / military ethics); chih ming self-sealing success-endorsement framework; Confucian virtue paradox

concepts.title: Chi — Spirit, Morale, and Manipulation
concepts.description: ch'i as manipulable variable (triple-drum doctrine, Ch'ang-chuo 684 BCE); diurnal ch'i cycle; Questions and Replies systematic treatment; T'ien Tan manufactured grievance as ch'i amplification mechanism

concepts.title: Fatal Terrain — Death Ground
concepts.description: nine ground types; the paradox (removing escape route maximizes commitment); Han Hsin Chao campaign as canonical demonstration; cognitive mechanism (eliminating false hope divides minds); Sun Pin extension

concepts.title: Thirty-Six Stratagems — Taxonomy of Unorthodox Operations
concepts.description: late Ming classification; chi (incipient moment) as organizing concept; six situation-groups; three exemplar cases (Mei-jen chi / K'ung-ch'eng chi / Wu-chung sheng-yu); T'ai-pai Yin-ching connection

concepts.title: Estrangement Techniques — Li-Chien
concepts.description: li-chien as formally theorized unorthodox sub-domain; Ch'en P'ing/Fan Tseng (203 BCE) paradigm case; Three Doubts framework; Ch'in six-state subversion; Chang Yi/Teng Hsiu jealousy exploitation; Eight Villains as internal estrangement; beauty agents as estrangement subcategory

concepts.title: Han Hsin — Three Paradigm Campaigns
concepts.description: Wei River (pontoon feint / jar-and-pole actual crossing), Chao (backs-to-river + red pennant seizure), Ch'i (dam reduction / feigned retreat / water release); zheng/qi as relational not categorical; Questions and Replies pedagogical use

concepts.title: Strategic Patience and Extended Deception
concepts.description: Li Mu multi-year identity construction vs. Hsiung-nu; compounding return (exponential not linear); T'ien Tan compressed parallel; fifth essential for victory; personal cost of deception as mechanism of convincingness

concepts.title: T'ien Tan — Eight-Stage Sequenced Operation
concepts.description: eight-stage reconstruction [PARAPHRASED]; ch'i management as structural spine; manufactured prisoner mutilation for grievance amplification; command authority as enabling condition for multi-stage sequential design

concepts.title: Systematic Subversion — Liu-t'ao Civil Offensive
concepts.description: five methods (bribe ministers / corrupt court / engineer economic exhaustion / buy information apparatus / amplify internal divisions); Three Doubts operational architecture; Ch'in vs. six-state alliance as 3rd-century BCE grand strategy application

concepts.title: Unrestricted Warfare — PRC Doctrine (1999)
concepts.description: Liang/Xiangsui Triple-S doctrine (supra-national, supra-domain, supra-means); three-entity model (military + para-military + non-military); Sawyer's 3,000-year continuity framing; Boot challenge; [INTERPRETIVE NOTE on contemporary strategic implications]

title: Chinese Military Theory (Sawyer, *The Tao of Spycraft*, 1998)

concepts.title: Knowing Men — Chih Jen
concepts.description: 2,500-year project for reading character from behavioral observation; Transparency Assumption + Constancy Principle; T'ai Kung's 15 discrepancies; Confucius three-step framework; King Wen's Six Indications as multi-modal assessment bridge; mourning rites as diagnostic; absence of Worthies indicator

concepts.title: Contrary Employment Doctrine
concepts.description: Hsü Tung's second-order intelligence epistemology: codified observation grammars become exploit targets; three levels of exploitation (manufacture false / suppress real / engineer ambiguity); expertise as exploitable predictability; self-undermining recursive structure

concepts.title: Field Observation Methods
concepts.description: passive intelligence grammar: dust taxonomy (chariot/infantry/foraging/camp signals); bird behavior as encampment indicator; flag/drum discipline; cookstove counts; feigned vs. real disorder; five situations of forbidden pursuit

concepts.title: Active Testing Protocols
concepts.description: applied chih jen methodology; Six Secret Teachings eight-method sequence (verbal depth → confound → collaborative analysis → explicit questioning → financial → sexual → crisis → drunkenness); Ta-Tai Li-chi ten-stage emotional sequence; structural principle: involuntary responses under designed pressure

concepts.title: Agent Qualification and Selection
concepts.description: five spy type qualification profiles; Li Ch'uan's roving agent addition not in Sun Tzu; handler quality as organizing variable; converted spy paradox; doomed spy doctrine (must not know status); compartmentalization as architectural response

concepts.title: Difficulties of Persuasion
concepts.description: Han Fei-tzu's Nan I: stated vs. actual preferences; target epistemology before argument selection; "magnify what they boast about, eliminate what they are ashamed of"; Li Ch'uan's six target types (benevolent/courageous/wise/stupid/menial/greedy); Eight Villains as sovereign-capture taxonomy

concepts.title: Ch'i as Military Intelligence Object
concepts.description: ch'i assessment as distinct intelligence function from manipulation; Ts'ao Kuei three-drum principle; Sun Pin's five-stage sequence (Expanding/Honing/Making Decisive/Exhaustion/Dissolution); Wu Ch'i desperation-ch'i distinction; fatal terrain as ch'i weapon; timing engagement to ch'i state

concepts.title: Assassination as State Instrument
concepts.description: three forms: physical / will / character assassination; Chuan Chu (dagger in fish, ceremony as delivery mechanism); Yao Li (self-sacrifice cover); Ching K'o's failed attempt as failure analysis; beauty agents as will assassination; agent selection as chih jen application

concepts.title: Systematic Covert Programs
concepts.description: Kou-chien's nine-technique campaign against Wu: sacred timber exhaustion / beauty agents / poisoned grain / financial inducements / Wu Yüan estrangement / training programs / artisan intelligence / rebellion support / patient timing; Chieh Hsüan's mu/obfuscation doctrinal framework; Li Ch'uan's coordination-across-time

concepts.title: Signs of a Doomed State
concepts.description: Han Fei-tzu's 35+ indicators (structural / personal behavioral / absence of Worthies / social behavioral); ironclad indicator: absence of Worthies = self-confirming failure to self-correct; Wei Liao-tzu doomed-city analysis; Pai Kuei's five exhaustion signs

concepts.title: Divination and Its Rejection
concepts.description: 3,000-year arc: Shang universality → Spring-Autumn selective acceptance → Wei Liao-tzu condemnation → Li Ching's cynical instrumentalization ("manipulate the greedy and stupid") → Northern Sung rationalist consolidation; strategic irrationalism as professional competency

title: Polar Exploration and Arctic History (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Franklin Expedition Lead Poisoning Failure
concepts.description: John Torrington lead levels 110-151 ppm bone, 600+ ppm hair; lead poisoning symptoms (insomnia, tremors, cognitive decline); lead-poisoned tinned food from Stephen Goldner hasty provisioning; cognitive compromise by 1847

concepts.title: Franklin Crew Forensic Analysis and Cannibalism
concepts.description: Captain James Fitzjames DNA identification (2014); cut marks on ribs, vertebrae, long bones; systematic butchering patterns; ~25% of recovered remains showing deliberate butchering

concepts.title: Franklin Expedition Historical Narrative
concepts.description: Lead poisoning → scurvy → starvation → organized cannibalism → death sequence; ships trapped in pack ice September 1846; abandoned April 1848; crew dispersal and systematic butchering for food

concepts.title: Arctic Exploration Failure and Adaptation
concepts.description: Environmental extremity; indigenous Arctic adaptation (Inuit, Yupik, Sámi); European refusal to adopt indigenous practices; inadequate provisioning, clothing, ships, knowledge; Franklin's refusal to adopt indigenous methods

title: Chinese Civilization Origins — Shamanism to State (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Shamanism and Chinese State Neolithic to Shang
concepts.description: 7,000-year arc from individual charisma (Peiligang shamanism) → institutionalized priesthood (Hongshan jade priests) → formalized divination (Longshan oracle bones) → state apparatus (Shang bureaucracy); phases: Peiligang/Shangshan (~8,000 BCE), Yangshao/Hongshan (~5,000 BCE), Longshan (~3,000 BCE), Taosi (~2,600 BCE), Shimao (~2,300 BCE), Erlitou/Erligang (~1900 BCE), Shang (~1200 BCE)

concepts.title: Peiligang Early Shamanism
concepts.description: 8,000-7,000 BCE; pit houses, stone tools, early pottery; egalitarian society with ritual specialists; personal authority based on demonstrated efficacy; no hereditary succession; limitation of shamanic authority (non-transferable); transition to institutional priesthood

concepts.title: Hongshan Ritual Landscape and Jade
concepts.description: 5,000-3,000 BCE; jade pig-dragons, combs, bi discs, cong cylinders; ritual deposits on hilltops; goddess imagery and female ritual specialists; jade as status marker and heritable authority; shift from personal shamanic to institutional priesthood

concepts.title: Taosi City Astronomy and State
concepts.description: 300-hectare walled city; astronomical platform aligned to solstices; knowledge-based political authority; shift from shamanism to astronomy to authority based on expertise; legendary Emperor Yao connection

concepts.title: Shimao Skull Sacrifice and Ritual Power
concepts.description: 400-hectare fortress, ~70 meter pyramid; 100+ female skulls beneath East Gate; violence evidence; shaman-king cosmology; ritual consolidation hypothesis; comparative human sacrifice (Egypt, Mesopotamia, China, Mesoamerica)

concepts.title: Longshan Oracle Bones Divination
concepts.description: Systematization of shamanic knowledge; inscribe question → apply heat → read cracks; standardized protocol; elite family control; Shang continuation with recording of outcomes; transition from charismatic to procedural authority

title: Neolithic and Prehistoric Violence (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Prehistoric Fortifications Eurasia LBK Amnya
concepts.description: LBK farmers (~5,500-4,500 BCE) and Amnya foragers (~6,500-4,500 BCE) both built fortifications; massacre sites (Talheim, Schöneck, Asparn, Halberstadt, Herxheim); ~10% of LBK skeletons show traumatic injuries; 8.2kya climate event driving territoriality; warfare correlates with settlement density, not subsistence type

concepts.title: Vittrup Man Mesolithic Slavery and Sacrifice
concepts.description: 1915 bog body discovery; 2024 genetic/isotopic analysis; maritime diet (seal/cod/whale) birth to age 13, then inland farmer diet; eight blunt-force skull blows (wooden club); ritual execution/sacrifice evidence; evidence of prehistoric slavery

concepts.title: Ritual Violence and State Formation
concepts.description: Human sacrifice at city founding, fortress construction, elite burial, military victory, political succession; absolute power demonstration; cosmological legitimation; captives, enemies, slaves as victims; transition to bureaucratic execution

title: Demographic and Population Studies (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Demographic Collapse Parsi
concepts.description: Population decline 100,000 (1950s) → 69,601 (2001) → ~60,000 (2011); 1-2% annual decline; fertility 0.8 vs. replacement 2.1; median age 42-43; extinction timeline 80-120 years; fertility collapse from education, economic opportunity, delayed marriage, voluntary childlessness; out-marriage 40-50% in diaspora; religious endogamy and no-conversion restrictions; aging population and generational collapse; diclofenac/vulture extinction disrupting sky burial; religious inflexibility

concepts.title: Tasmania Aboriginal Dietary Collapse
concepts.description: 43,000-10,000 years ago megafauna present; 10,000-3,500 years ago transition period; 3,500 years ago to contact restricted diet; ocean-going technology abandonment; climate change 3,500 years ago; dietary narrowing and technological loss; isolation hypothesis

concepts.title: Iceland Decodegen Genetics Patenting
concepts.description: 1,000-year population isolation; 1998 health sector license granting access to genetic samples, genealogical data, medical records; consent and re-identification controversies; 2003 reforms (opt-out, profit-sharing); discovery outcomes; genetic patriotism framing; comparison to genetic colonialism

title: Maritime Power and Trade Networks (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Thalassocracy and Maritime Power
concepts.description: Maritime dominance as political power; prerequisites (ship design, navigation, infrastructure, naval capability); tributary networks and monopoly pricing; spice trade example; exploration and first-mover advantage

title: Inuit Mythology and Arctic Culture (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Tuunbaq Mythology Predator Symbol
concepts.description: Predatory entity in Inuit cosmology; Arctic predators (polar bears, orca whales, walrus); composite threat encoding; predator as cosmic principle; humans as prey in Arctic environment; ritual protocols for hunting and avoiding predators; mythology as psychological framework

title: Spanish Conquest of Mexico (Townsend / *The Fifth Sun*, 2003)

concepts.title: Conquest as Negotiated Process
concepts.description: Conquest was contingent on specific indigenous choices (Marina's translation, Moctezuma's negotiation, Tlaxcaltec alliance) not inevitable Spanish military superiority; Spanish military advantage was real but made decisive only through indigenous political divisions and pragmatic choices; Spanish success required indigenous labor, intermediaries, and continued functioning of indigenous institutions

concepts.title: Marina as Indispensable Agent of Conquest
concepts.description: Enslaved indigenous woman whose translation made Spanish conquest possible; translation was essential cognitive labor constructing meaning across incommensurable worldviews; systematic erasure from conquest narratives reveals how translator labor is made invisible and uncompensated; Marina's invisibility serves ideological function maintaining Spanish narrative authority

concepts.title: Smallpox as Force Multiplier Not Cause
concepts.description: Epidemic disease cascading through multiple systems simultaneously (succession crises, labor disruption, burial obligations, decision-making paralysis); smallpox enabled conquest but did not determine it; simultaneous disruption at social, political, and economic levels created crisis management failure

concepts.title: Moctezuma's Pragmatism Under Radical Uncertainty
concepts.description: Pragmatic negotiating strategy in response to unprecedented threat, incomplete intelligence, institutional constraints; negotiation was rational choice minimizing catastrophic risk while preserving institutional structures; choice enabled Spanish to exploit political divisions and achieve conquest

concepts.title: Xiuhpohualli: 52-Year Indigenous Calendar as Political Framework
concepts.description: Cyclical time structure framing conquest as disruption within ongoing cycle rather than apocalypse; indigenous use of calendar as resistance to Spanish linear chronology; temporal autonomy as political assertion

concepts.title: Altepetl: Indigenous City-States as Political Units
concepts.description: Altepetl as persistent administrative units surviving conquest through Spanish pragmatic preservation; Spanish preserved structures because they were functionally efficient; indigenous institutions became foundation of colonial administration

concepts.title: Calpolli: Decentralized Administration Through Kinship Structure
concepts.description: Kinship-based labor organization as remarkably efficient administrative technology; Spanish preserved calpolli because colonial system depended on it; indigenous people maintained structures partly for community preservation, partly enabling Spanish extraction through their own institutions

concepts.title: Tlatoani: Paramount Chief as Negotiator Under Constraint
concepts.description: Tlatoani preserved elite identity by negotiating position within Spanish colonial hierarchy; pragmatic choice for status preservation despite loss of sovereignty; elite adaptation demonstrates how institutional roles survive political disruption

concepts.title: Flower Wars (Xochiyaoyotl): Ritualized Conflict as Sophisticated Statecraft
concepts.description: Ritualized military practice appearing as weakness in Spanish frame but wisdom in indigenous frame; Spanish refusal to follow protocols prevented indigenous ability to communicate military capacity through established channels; system collapsed not from indigenous weakness but from Spanish protocol refusal

concepts.title: Chimalpahin's Annals: Indigenous Historiography as Archive and Assertion
concepts.description: Indigenous historian using documentation as resistance strategy; preserving indigenous perspective, genealogy, and knowledge against Spanish colonization threat; archive as political act

title: Spanish Conquest and Colonial Analysis (Townsend / *The Fifth Sun*, 2023)

concepts.title: Translator Invisibility: The Hidden Scaffolding of Historic Change
concepts.description: translation labor foundational yet structurally invisible; Marina's case as paradigm: absolutely essential and systematically erased; invisibility serves ideological function for colonial narratives of autonomous European agency; behavioral economics of invisible cognitive labor

concepts.title: Agency vs. Structure: How Individual Choices Navigate Institutional Inevitability
concepts.description: agency and structure not opposites; structure sets possible options, agency selects which to pursue; conquest contingent on specific indigenous choices (Marina's translation, Moctezuma's negotiation, Tlaxcaltec alliance) within structural constraints (Spanish military advantage)

concepts.title: Pragmatism vs. Ideology: How Practical Choices Get Reinterpreted as Inevitable Beliefs
concepts.description: pragmatic choices systematically reinterpreted as ideological stances by outside observers with narrative authority; Moctezuma's negotiation reframed from rational-response-to-constraint to fatalistic-surrender; interpretation transforms contingent outcomes into inevitable consequences of indigenous beliefs

concepts.title: Archive as Resistance: Documentation as Political Act Under Colonialism
concepts.description: archive not neutral preservation but political choice about whose perspective matters; Chimalpahin's Nahuatl annals as resistance — indigenous documentation in form Spanish crown recognized while preserving indigenous meaning structures; archive as assertion of persistence and legitimacy

concepts.title: Agricultural Development Timing: How Material Preconditions Enable or Constrain Political Possibility
concepts.description: sophisticated agricultural development (chinampas, tribute networks) created material foundation enabling Mexica civilization AND vulnerability Spanish exploited; path dependence in agricultural systems constrained strategic options under Spanish invasion; sophistication as simultaneous advantage and liability

title: Farmer-Forager Contact (Stone Age Herbalist / *Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III*, 2023)

concepts.title: Farmer-Forager Contact Dynamics: From Narrative of Progress to Ecology of Choice
concepts.description: three divergent outcomes from farmer-forager contact (Corded Ware Baltic: de-neolithisation; Drakensberg: hybridity; Mediterranean: coexistence); outcomes depend on ecology and cultural choice, not agricultural superiority as universal principle; agricultural progress not inevitable
